# Copilot Instructions

## Repository Structure

```
src/                     # Feature source code
  <feature-name>/
    devcontainer-feature.json   # Feature metadata and options
    install.sh                  # Main install script (runs as root at build time)
    [runtime-helper.sh]         # Optional runtime scripts (onCreateCommand, postStartCommand, etc.)

test/                    # Tests
  <feature-name>/
    test.sh              # Integration test (runs inside a container)
    scenarios.json       # Optional scenario matrix for parameterised tests
    [scenario-name].sh   # Test script for each scenario
  unit/
    test_<feature>.sh    # Unit tests (run without Docker, fast)

.github/
  workflows/
    test.yml             # CI: unit tests + integration tests
    lint.yml             # shellcheck + shfmt
    release.yaml         # Publishes features to ghcr.io
```

## Feature Anatomy

Every feature must have:
1. `devcontainer-feature.json` — id, version, name, description, options
2. `install.sh` — runs as root during image build; must be idempotent

Optional runtime scripts (referenced via `onCreateCommand`, `postStartCommand`, etc.):
- Installed to `/usr/local/bin/<helper-name>` during build
- Must handle failures gracefully (don't crash the container)

## MANDATORY: Unit Tests

Every feature **must** have unit tests in `test/unit/` that run without Docker.

- File naming: `test_<feature_id_with_underscores>.sh`
- Tests use PATH manipulation to mock external commands (curl, apt-get, docker, etc.)
- Scripts with functions and a `BASH_SOURCE` guard can be sourced directly
- All unit tests must produce `Results: N passed, 0 failed`

## Code Style

- **Indentation**: tabs (enforced by shfmt)
- **Linting**: `shellcheck --severity=warning` — all scripts must pass
- **Formatting**: `shfmt --diff` — no diff = pass
- **Error handling**: `set -e` (sh scripts) or `set -euo pipefail` (bash scripts)
- **BASH_SOURCE guard**: bash scripts with reusable functions use `if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then ... fi` so they can be sourced for unit testing

## Running Tests Locally

```bash
# Unit tests (no Docker required)
bash test/unit/test_git_identity.sh
bash test/unit/test_devbox_install.sh
bash test/unit/test_oh_my_zsh_install.sh
bash test/unit/test_supabase_cli_install.sh
bash test/unit/test_coder_install.sh

# Lint all shell scripts
find . -not -path './.git/*' -name '*.sh' -print0 | xargs -0 shellcheck --severity=warning
find . -not -path './.git/*' -name '*.sh' -print0 | xargs -0 shfmt --diff

# Integration tests (requires Docker + devcontainer CLI)
npm install -g @devcontainers/cli
devcontainer features test --skip-scenarios -f <feature-name> -i mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:ubuntu-22.04 .
devcontainer features test --skip-autogenerated --skip-duplicated -f <feature-name> .
```
